summary: twilio-sdk postinstall exfiltrates host, AD, CI, and /etc/hosts data to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: Package name `twilio-sdk` impersonates the legitimate `twilio` npm package; index.js exports an empty object and ships no SDK functionality. On `npm install`, scripts.postinstall runs postinstall.js which collects hostname and FQDN, Active Directory signals (USERDNSDOMAIN, USERDOMAIN, LOGONSERVER), USERNAME, USERPROFILE, OneDrive corporate path, detected VPN client, the first 20 non-loopback entries from /etc/hosts, and CI repository identifiers (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_*, CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, JENKINS_URL, npm_config_registry), then transmits them as query-string parameters via plain-HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping. The README claims 'No data is collected,' which directly contradicts the postinstall behavior. The combination of typosquat naming, empty stub library code, automatic install-time beacon to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint, and harvesting of corporate/AD/CI reconnaissance data is a classic dependency-confusion reconnaissance attack against any developer or build system that mistypes the real `twilio` package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
